title: Grab eyes growth amid fuel crunch
desc: Grab Thailand is bracing for a "mini-crisis" due to rising fuel costs, with a contingency plan to introduce passenger surcharges while sustaining aggressive promotions to maintain demand and merchant income, particularly as more people work from home.

title: Commission opts to tighten data centre supervision
desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) is tightening its regulatory grip on the booming data centre sector through a proposed shift in the licensing regime that will bring operators under closer state scrutiny.

title: SME loan demand deteriorates in Q1
desc: Confidence among small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) fell sharply in the first quarter, driven by mounting concerns over rising costs linked to the energy crisis and a slowdown in lending, according to the latest survey by the SME Development Bank of Thailand (SME D Bank).

title: Crackdown targets use of Thai proxies
desc: The government is tightening controls on nominees by introducing new measures to prevent foreigners from using Thais as proxies to operate businesses, effective April 1.

title: CAAT moves to ease flight prices as Songkran looms
desc: The Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand (CAAT) is stepping up oversight of airfares amid disruptions linked to the situation in the Middle East, and is working with six airlines to cut ticket prices during the upcoming Songkran holiday by 15–30% while adding more than 29,000 seats over this busy period.

title: CPF vows to keep food prices unchanged
desc: Charoen Pokphand Foods (CPF) Plc, a leading integrated agro-industrial and food conglomerate, says it will keep its meat and food prices unchanged for at least two months as the company uses local sources for most of its raw materials.

title: Govt eyes fuel tax reduction
desc: The government may be forced to implement fuel tax reductions if the Oil Fuel Fund proves insufficient to stabilise domestic prices, Deputy Prime Minister Ekniti Nitithanprapas has warned.

title: MFA proposes slashing visa-free tourist period in half
desc: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has proposed reducing the visa-free tourist period from 60 days to 30 days, citing national security concerns.

title: Opposition seeks energy crisis debate
desc: Opposition parties agreed at their first informal whips' meeting on Tuesday to seek an urgent House debate on the country's energy crisis, with the People's Party (PP) warning it would expose alleged fuel hoarding during today's parliamentary session.

title: New cabinet ready for royal endorsement
desc: The new cabinet line-up is ready to be submitted for royal endorsement, Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said on Tuesday while downplaying reports on the absence of legal scholar Borwornsak Uwanno.

title: Haad Thip turns  to returnable glass
desc: SET-listed Haad Thip Plc, the exclusive bottler and distributor for Coca-Cola in 14 southern provinces, plans to expand its glass-bottled products to mitigate risks from rising plastic pellet prices, driven by ongoing conflicts in the Middle East.

title: Middle East conflict delays auto exports to key market
desc: Thailand's automotive industry is facing mounting pressure as the Israel-US war on Iran disrupts shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz, delaying vehicle exports to the Middle East, says the Federation of Thai Industries (FTI).

title: AXA highlights local insurance protection gap
desc: Thailand needs to address the protection gap in the insurance industry to drive growth in the sector, which is critical for the country's economic development and the well-being of its people, says France-based insurer AXA.

title: Island to host global meet
desc: Phuket: The island province is set to host the Global Sustainable Tourism Conference 2026 (GSTC 2026) from April 21–24, according to the Tourism Authority of Thailand (TAT).

title: Gas price hike to push up Thai power bills
desc: The new government is bracing for a surge in electricity bills as liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices climb in the wake of conflict in the Middle East, according to an energy official who requested anonymity.

title: Shipments rise in February on industrial demand
desc: Exports surged by 9.9% year-on-year to US$29.4 billion in February, driven by strong industrial goods shipments, while imports jumped by 31.8% to $32.3 billion, mainly due to machinery and gold purchases, according to the Trade Policy and Strategy Office (TPSO).

title: Crackdown on illegal work results in over 140 arrests
desc: More than 140 foreign nationals were arrested between March 16 and 22 in a sweeping crackdown on illegal employment in Chon Buri, immigration authorities said on Tuesday.

title: Australian tourist suffers mysterious head injuries in Pattaya Walking Street pub
desc: Tourist police respond to an incident on Pattaya’s Walking Street after an Australian man was found with multiple head injuries following a disturbance inside a pub. PATTAYA, Thailand – An Australian tourist was left with multiple head injuries after a late-night incident inside a pub on Pattaya’s Walking Street, prompting police to investigate the circumstances surrounding the case. Tourist police stationed along Walking Street received a report around 11:00 p.m. on March 23 that a foreign visitor had been injured inside an entertainment venue in South Pattaya area. Officers rushed to the sce

title: Senate split over Constitution Drafting Assembly plan
desc: The Senate has found itself divided over a proposal to establish a 200-member Constitution Drafting Assembly (CDA) to draft a new charter, with senators expressing both support and opposition during the latest parliamentary session.

title: Pattaya Immigration arrests 146 illegal foreign workers, revokes 250 visas
desc: Immigration officers conduct checks along Pattaya Beach and Second Road, targeting foreign workers operating without proper permits. PATTAYA, Thailand – Chonburi immigration authorities have intensified enforcement against illegal foreign workers in Pattaya and surrounding areas, arresting 146 individuals and revoking 250 visas in a sweeping operation aimed at protecting Thai jobs. The crackdown, led by Police Major General Songprod Sirisukha, Commander of Immigration Division 3, and his senior team, focused on Pattaya Beach Road and Second Road. Officers targeted foreign nationals working wit

title: L.P.N. to buy B2bn of land for Bangkok condo development
desc: SET-listed L.P.N. Development plans to spend 1-2 billion baht on new land acquisitions for condo development after freezing purchases for more than two years to clear inventory and refocus on the upper-affordable segment.

title: SSF reform 'urgent' as society ages
desc: People's Party (PP) MP Nattacha Boonchaiinsawat has called for reforms to the Social Security Fund (SSF) to make it more transparent and sustainable, and to allow members to have a say in how the fund is managed.

title: Fuel shortages threaten Thailand’s fisheries, raising concerns for Pattaya’s seafood tables
desc: Fishing boats sit idle in Pattaya Bay as soaring fuel costs and supply shortages threaten to halt operations, putting the city’s seafood supply at risk.

title: Thailand slashes Songkran airfares up to 30% despite soaring fuel costs
desc: CAAT and six domestic airlines, including Thai Airways and Bangkok Airways, offer nearly 30,000 discounted seats for April 10–15 travel.

title: Pattaya officials raid illegal vape sales operation as owner confronts law enforcers
desc: Pattaya officials inspect hundreds of illegal electronic cigarettes found in the trunk of a car during a raid on a vape sales operation in Nong Prue.

title: Foreign tourists brawl on Pattaya street before shocking stunt, then shake hands and walk away
desc: Two foreign tourists exchange blows in Soi 16 on Pattaya Second Road before the fight abruptly ends with a handshake.

title: Sunny skies and calm seas expected in Pattaya as hot weather persists
desc: Speed boats glide across Pattaya Bay under clear skies, with calm seas and mild waves offering ideal conditions for boating.

title: Man wounded in neck after stranger joins drinking session in Pattaya
desc: A late-night drinking session turned violent when a man was wounded in the neck during an altercation with a stranger in Pattaya.

title: Thai diesel hits 33 baht as subsidy cut responds to soaring global oil prices
desc: PTT and Bangchak raise gasoline, gasohol, and diesel prices, with diesel hitting the 33 THB ceiling.

title: Thailand says shorter visa-free stays won’t disrupt tourism sector
desc: Thailand is considering slashing visa-free stays for tourists from 60 to 30 days to bolster national security.

title: Thai tanker passes through Hormuz safely
desc: A Thai ship has passed safely through the Strait of Hormuz following successful negotiations with Iran and Oman, Foreign Affairs Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said on Tuesday.

title: Compensation awarded in Thai gold mine pollution case
desc: The Civil Court has ruled in favour of 382 residents of Phichit and Phetchabun provinces, ordering the operator of the Chatree gold mine operator to pay compensation of up to 200,000 baht per person, and to restore contaminated waterways after finding its operations caused harmful pollution and health impacts.

title: Army intelligence warns Cambodia is stockpiling weapons
desc: The Thai army intelligence chief has warned that the border conflict with Cambodia could reignite, pointing out that Phnom Penh has placed large orders for weaponary.

title: Pheu Thai leader aims to double seat total in 2030
desc: Pheu Thai Party leader Julapun Amornvivat has called on party MPs to remain united and set their sights on returning 150 members to the House in the next election, while dismissing speculation that members were jockeying for cabinet seats.

title: Akaryn Hotel Group launches akyra Bangkok 11
desc: The akyra Bangkok 11 is Akaryn Hotel Group’s latest lifestyle hotel, a smart, design-forward haven in the heart of Sukhumvit that balances Bangkok’s energy with genuine calm.

title: A decade of Lancang-Mekong cooperation: forging ahead together toward a shared future
desc: This article was written by Wang Yi , Foreign Minister of the People's Republic of China. The Lancang-Mekong nourishes our six nations and binds us closely together.

title: Two Thai students killed in Miami hit-and-run crash
desc: MIAMI, US — Two Thai nationals were killed in a hit-and-run crash in Miami Beach during a spring break trip, authorities in the United States said.

title: Iran says Thai vessel crosses Hormuz safely, vows not to “forget its friends”
desc: The Iranian Embassy in Bangkok said a Thai vessel has passed through the Strait of Hormuz safely, following close coordination between Iran, Thailand and Oman.

desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，据泰媒报道，为应对即将到来的宋干节出行高峰，泰国民航局宣布，已联合六家主要航空公司推出国内航线机票降价及运力提升措施。

desc: This article was written by Zhang Jianwei , Chinese Ambassador to Thailand. The Lancang-Mekong River binds the peoples of the six Lancang-Mekong countries closely together.

title: French man reports assault, 30,000 baht bill at Chiang Mai karaoke
desc: French man reports assault, 30,000 baht bill at Chiang Mai karaoke Legacy Police in Mueang Chiang Mai are investigating a complaint filed yesterday, March 23, after a Frenchman reported an assault and a 30,000 baht drinks bill at a karaoke venue in Chiang Mai. The tourist gave a statement identifying himself as 35 year old Kevin, a French national who has been living in Chon Buri province for around two years. According to him, the bill included eight small bottles of beer and service fees, totalling 30,000 baht. Kevin told investigators the argument over the bill escalated into pushing and pu

title: 与泰自豪党政策协商顺利，育查南透露双方将共同推动落实
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国为泰党名单议员育查南在谈及油价持续上涨对民众生活的影响时表示，为泰党已准备好在明天（3月25日）的会议上提出相关议案，在上次会议时为泰党其实就已准备好了，这次可以直接推进。同时，为泰党各地区的议员也在收集各自选区民众的声音。最近油价一次调涨了2泰铢/升，让全国民众普遍感到压力，今天议员们将充分交换各地区民众所面临的困境，并持续关注每天的局势变化。 在外交事务方面，为泰党一直尽力照顾海外泰国人。由于过去为泰党曾负责过外交部，所以仍保留了与海外泰国人联系的网络，能及时收到他们在国外遇到的问题并向上反馈。 至于昨天为泰党代表与泰自豪党进行的政策协商，双方谈得非常顺利。为泰党已将希望在4年内解决的问题、短期措施和之前竞选时的政策主张，都纳入到政府政策中，泰自豪党也表示愿意把这些内容加入施政计划，一起推动落实。 关于党内的KPI考核，今天的党内会议也会进行讨论。这套考核标准将用来评估为泰党所制定的政策是否真正可行，分为两个部分：1. 地方实际执行情况，2. 整体表现。鉴于为泰党目前负责的是社会 and 人力资源相关的部门，成果可能无法在短期内显现，但如果做得好，可以解决泰国的长期问题和结构性难题。 育查南补充说，当外部环境改变时，党的一些中期政策或需要调整为短期政策。但就现在的情况看，为泰党在科学与技术方面的政策与泰自豪党结合得很好，因为这一块的内容非常明确，除了是

title: 泰国4月1日起实行新规，从严打击“人头公司”
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国商业部商业发展厅厅长普蓬透露，商业发展厅已于3月16日发布了合伙公司与有限公司登记办公室第1/2569号命令，针对合伙企业增加外国人为合伙人、或有限公司增加外国人为有权签字董事的情况，新增严格管控措施，以更有效地防止外国人利用泰国“代持人”作为掩护（即“人头公司”）在泰经营业务。 新措施要求各公司负责签署注册申请的执行合伙人或董事，必须出具书面确认书，声明所有合伙人或股东都已实际出资且支付了股款，且没有以任何形式帮助、支持或与外国人共同从事“人头公司”性质的业务。 普蓬表示，之所以追加这项措施，是因为今年1月1日商业发展厅已出台命令，要求外国人持股未达50%或有外国人担任有权签字董事的“高风险人头公司”在注册时，必须提交泰国股东的银行对账单来验证实际出资。虽然该命令已让疑似“人头公司”的注册申请减少了65%，但后来发现仍有人设法规避，因此再次推出更加严格的补充命令。 根据新命令，注册申请人提交确认书后，商业发展厅将筛选出风险人名单，并移交给国家警察总署中央调查局（CIB）逐一进行审查。同时，商业发展厅提醒所有公司，提供虚假信息将触犯《刑法》第137条和第267条的规定，最高可判处6个月有期徒刑，或罚款最高1万泰铢，或两者并罚；情节严重者最高可判处3年有期徒刑，或罚款最高6万泰铢，或两者并罚。另外，还可能违反1999版《外国人经营法》第36条，最高可

title: 美国人滞留泰国素万那普机场8天？假的！
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月23日，近日网络流传的一段视频引发了不少关注，一名外国人称他已经在泰国素万那普机场住了8天多，原因是中东战事推高机票价格，他买不起回程机票，要求航空公司承担费用，否则他就继续留在机场不走。移民局第二分局局长卡塔通警少将就此回应说，视频中的男子是美国人，3月15日从新加坡飞抵泰国，但一到机场就被移民局检察人员拒绝入境，因为从他的行程安排和整体状况来看，并不像普通游客。按照规定，载他来的航空公司必须先行照顾，并为他安排后续飞往最终目的地的航班。 然而，这名男子没有钱购买下一段机票，只能暂时留在机场。移民局强调，该男子所说的“受战事影响”是否属实无法确认，但经过查证，他计划从泰国启程前往的目的地并非任何处于战争状态的国家，而是东南亚某国，之后他将再次转机返回美国。另外，这名男子已于3月21日离开泰国。 卡塔通警少将指出，移民局的职责在完成拒绝入境命令后就已结束，之后的一切安排，包括购买机票和送这名美籍男子离开，都是航空公司的责任。在他滞留机场的几天里，航空公司没有置之不理，而是按照规定提供了必要的照顾。现在他把视频发到社交媒体，像是在刻意制造话题，或许是为了增加点击量，博取同情以募集捐款。卡塔通警少将提醒民众，不要轻易被这类内容打动，更不希望泰国人被这种人利用。 （编译：KA；来源：ch3plus） The post 美国人滞留泰国素万那普机场8天？假的！ appe
